title: Meet the high class: ข ฉ ถ ฐ ผ
pedagogy: High-class consonants (1/3): aspirated stops ข ฉ ถ ฐ ผ — breathy counterparts of mid-class ก จ ต ป. Concepts: what consonant classes are for; the high-class sound profile.

exercises.title: Why consonant classes matter
body: Every Thai consonant belongs to one of three classes: mid, high, or low. The class doesn't change how the consonant sounds — it changes the tone of the syllable it starts.
You already know the nine mid-class letters. This unit adds the eleven high-class ones.
Learn each letter's class along with its sound. You'll need both when tones arrive in the Tone Rules unit.

exercises.title: The high-class sound
exercises.thaiExample: ข ฉ ถ ผ
body: High-class consonants are aspirated or breathy: kh, ch, th, ph, f, s, h — said with a puff of air.
Many are the airy siblings of a mid-class letter: ข (kh) puffs up ก (k), ถ (th) puffs up ต (t), ผ (ph) puffs up ป (p).
If a consonant feels breathy, suspect high class.
